items.title: War, Inflation Can’t Stop ETFs’ Trillion-Dollar Inflow Boom
items.summary: Exchange-traded funds are attracting cash at a record-breaking trillion-dollar pace despite persistent inflation, war in the Middle East and bouts of market volatility — a testament to American households’ enduring buy-and-hold conviction.

items.title: AtkinsRealis Gains on Canada’s Expanded Nuclear Strategy
items.summary: AtkinsRealis Group Inc. extended a rally on Monday after the Canadian government announced plans to expand its nuclear program, turning analysts more bullish on the Montreal-based infrastructure engineering firm.

items.title: Trump Says Iran Will Agree to Have Major Weapons Inspections
items.summary: “Everybody is fully aware that Iran will agree to have Major Weapons Inspections in order to ensure “Nuclear Honesty” long into the future,” President Trump writes in a post on Truth Social. Heather Conley, nonresident senior fellow at the American Enterprise Institute, joined Balance of Power to discuss the recent news from the Middle East. She said Iran is being paid to open the Strait of Hormuz

items.title: RT by @mfa_russia: 🕯️ On June 22 in Netanya, Ambassador of Russia to Israel Anatoly Viktorov took part in a wreath- and flower-laying ceremony at the Monument to the Red Army’s Victory over Nazi Germany to mark the Day of Remembrance and Sorrow. In his address Anatoly Viktorov recalled the treacherous attack by Nazi Germany on the USSR on June 22,1941, and the horrendous atrocities committed by the Nazis in the occupied Soviet territories. He drew attention to the new commemorative date established in Russia, April 19 – the Day of Remembrance for the Victims of the Genocide of the Soviet People, perpetrated by the Nazis and their henchmen during the Great Patriotic War. He emphasized that the Soviet people of all nationalities, despite the unimaginable suffering, did not remain passive victims of the Nazi aggressors, but instead treaded a difficult path from the tragic June 22 to the victorious May 9. He noted the particular importance of preserving the memory of the Soviet people’s steadfastness and courage amid pushy attempts by some countries to downplay the Soviet Union`s decisive contribution to the defeat of Nazism and the end of the horrors of the Holocaust. He pointed out the shared duty of the peoples of Russia and Israel to pass on to future generations the truth about that war and to continue resolutely countering the course toward historical revisionism and revanchism adopted by a number of countries, as well as the glorification of Nazi executioners and their Ukrainian and other henchmen. He noted that our enemies in the West have once again imposed war upon us by supporting the criminal Kiev junta and attempting, through its actions, to inflict a “strategic defeat” on Russia. He assured that all the objectives of the special military operation would be achieved, as Russia’s security, national interests and future are being resolutely and selflessly defended by our modern heroes – the participants of the SMO. He added that Russia has not ruled out a diplomatic solution that would address the root causes of the conflict. The event was attended by Head of the Bureau for Eurasian and Western Balkan Affairs at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Israel Liat Wexelman, Ambassador of Belarus Yury Yaroshevich, Ambassador of Uzbekistan Jakhongir Aminov, Chargé d’Affaires of Kazakhstan in Israel Arman Baysuanov, representatives of the Russian House in Israel, the Russian Ecclesiastical Mission in Jerusalem, Coordinating Council of Organizations of Russian-Speaking Compatriots in Israel, Union of World War II Veterans – Fighters Against Nazism, Public Association “Strength in Unity”, “Israeli Anti-Fascist Movement”, All-Israel Association “Bridge of Memory,” All-Israel Association of Russian Compatriot Communities, Association “ASCEND”, diplomats, compatriots and the media. On the same day, diplomats from Russia and Belarus laid flowers in Jerusalem at the “Candle of Memory” Monument to the heroic defenders and residents of besieged Leningrad. Also in attendance were representatives of the Russian House in Israel, the Russian Ecclesiastical Mission in Jerusalem, Association of Holocaust Survivors and Rescuers, Jerusalem Association of Holocaust Survivors, Union of World War II Veterans – Fighters Against Nazism, Public Association “Strength in Unity”. Participants in both events observed a minute of silence to honor the memory of all victims of the Nazi criminals and the soldiers who fell on the battlefields of the Great Patriotic War.
items.summary:

items.title: RT by @mfa_russia: Commemorative Ceremony in Brussels on the Occasion of the Day of Remembrance and Sorrow 🗓 On 22 June 2026, on the occasion of the Day of Remembrance and Sorrow and the 85th anniversary of the beginning of the Great Patriotic War, diplomats of the Permanent Mission of Russia to the EU laid flowers at the graves of Soviet citizens who fell in the struggle against Nazism in Europe, as well as joined the Candle of Memory international campaign. The initiative was first launched in 2009 and ever since, candles have been lit across the world every year on this tragic day in memory of the 27 million Soviet citizens who died during the Great Patriotic War of 1941–1945. 🕯We bow our heads in memory of all those who gave their lives for the Motherland and will always remember our heroes. No one is forgotten, nothing is forgotten.

items.title: Dennis Lockhart on Greenspan's Legacy, Warsh's Path Ahead
items.summary: Alan Greenspan, the former Federal Reserve chairman, has died at the age of 100 due to complications of Parkinson’s disease. Greenspan served as Fed chief for 18 years, from 1987 to 2006, and was known for guiding a record US economic expansion, but his legacy was later dimmed by the financial crisis that erupted after he stepped down.
